Petition For Approval Of 1. The Settlement Agreement; 2. Withdrawal Of Funds From The Estate Blocked Account; And 3. Irrevocable Gift To Don Nguyen
Off Calendar Sep-02-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: David F. Myers appeared by CourtCall for Suretec Insurance Company. Edward T. Weber appeared by CourtCall for Thao Thi Lan Nguyen (Administrator). Diem Pham appeared by Zoom for Son Nguyen.

The Court stated that before it today are six petitions and that it would start with the sixth petition on calendar which is Son Nguyen's Petition for Approval of 1) The Settlement Agreement; 2) Withdrawal of Funds from the Estate's Blocked Account; and 3) Irrevocable Gift to Don Nguyen. The Court stated that the matter would be taken under submission and that it would be granted as submitted. Ms. Pham stated that there was no objection. The Court expressed one reservation with regard to the 664.6 request stating that request is usually to the court not to a particular department in the court and inquired if that language/request was intentional. Ms. Pham confirmed that it was an intentional request noting that they wanted this department/court to retain jurisdiction, that in sixty days the estate could be closed, that this department/court should retain jurisdiction given the facts, and that the request was also based on the length of time/the six years that this matter has been ongoing. No objection was raised by any other party. The Court stated that it would take the remaining five petitions off calendar. Ms. Pham stated that the remaining five petitions could be continued 60-70 days. The Court stated that it was not inclined to continue the remaining petitions 60-70 days. There being no objection, the Court ordered the following remaining petitions Off Calendar: 1) Administrator and Respondent Thao Thi Lan Nguyen's Petition for Order for Reimbursement; 2)Son Nguyen's Petition for Enforcement of Judgment, etc.; 3) Administrator's Petition for Waiver of Bond; 4) Administrator Thao Thi Lan Nguyen's Petition for Final Distribution; and 5) Son Nguyen's Petition for Orders Compelling Thao Thi Lan Nguyen: 1. To File Corrected Inventory and Appraisal, etc.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Matter not reported.

Off Calendar Jan-14-2020 Continued To Feb-25-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Diem Pham for Son Nguyen.
Lyndon Chee for Suretec Insurance Company.
Edward Weber appeared by telephone for Thao Nguyen.

The Court noted that there is a total of five petitions on calendar, that there are a number of procedural issues with various petitions as well as substantive issues that need to be addressed, stated that the Administrator's petitions have been signed by the Attorney and not the Administrator, and that Son's petition has not been verified. Ms. Pham stated that there has been no communication back from Mr. Weber's office, that she received no responses to phone calls or e-mails, and proposed removing the Administrator and appointing a Neutral. The Court noted that there is no petition before it seeking removal. Mr. Weber stated that since the last hearing there was a loss in communication with the Administrator, that the Administrator moved and he was not aware of it, and that the Administrator has proposed an agreement, but it was not verified. Mr. Weber also stated that he intends to get the verification and move the matter forward, and that he will provide the offer to Ms. Pham right away. The Court asks if the Parties are interested in mediation. Ms. Pham stated that they did mediation twice and that they came to an agreement, but the Administrator refused to sign it. The Petition for Orders Compelling Thao Nguyen to File Corrected Inventory and Appraisal, etc., Petition for Order for Reimbursement, Petition for Final Distribution, etc. Petition for Waiver of Bond and Petition for Enforcement of Judgment, etc. are continued to February 25, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the expectation that Mr. Weber's Client's proposal get communicated to Ms. Pham to open some dialogue for the Parties to see if this can be negotiated, or file any additional petitions if needed. The Parties are to file a joint status statement by February 18, 2020. All of the procedural issue and the substantive issues are to be taken care of.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

The Court informed Mr. Nguyen that the petition is not quite right, stated that he is asking for transfer of 100% of the property, that they now have the issue of the child born after death and that they may have to probate. Mr. Nguyen asked for a continuance to file a third amended petition and re-notice, stated that he was informed of another biological Brother living in Canada and that he filed a petition for a Guardian Ad Litem. The Court also informed Counsel that a guardianship might be needed, stated that it was okay to file the third amended petition but that he should only ask for 50% of the property to go to the Spouse, that he should not ask to transfer the credit cards, the credit line or the mortgage and that a life insurance policy if any passes outside of the petition. The 2nd Amended Spousal Property Petition was continued to July 22, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)
